projects.name: Greenwich Dental Group — Website Redesign & Support
projects.nextMilestone: Client review-ready by Jul 20 → revisions → go live ~Aug 2

iterations.note: Project opened: website redesign plus ongoing support engagement.

Background: site was migrated from WordPress to GoHighLevel ~1 year ago due to WordPress performance issues. GDG now wants a full redesign modeled on Apa Aesthetic's site (apaaesthetic.com style — clean, minimal, editorial imagery, dark/light section contrast). Budget negotiated and agreed at $7,500 for the rebuild/redesign. Steven has a Google Doc spec covering: homepage structure modeled on Greenwich Polo Club nav, doctor bio hover-to-color interactions, team/culture/services/locations/gallery/testimonials/internship/patient-info tabs, a future 'Dental Technology' tab, B-roll footage from Jonathan for the hero, Downtown Greenwich vs Old Greenwich location landing pages with office-specific doctor lists, and consultation forms that route to the correct office email based on page/dropdown selection. Keep GDG's existing blue brand color throughout.

Timeline set: waiting on 50% deposit before officially starting. Once paid — ~2-3 weeks to build to a comfortable state, then ~1 week of revisions with GDG and team, targeting ~1 month total to go live (~Aug 2).

Added SEO tasks: (1) set up URL redirects in GoHighLevel from old URLs to new URLs so nothing breaks/loses ranking on relaunch, and (2) add meta tags, meta descriptions, and page titles across every page for SEO.
50% deposit ($3,750) received. Project officially started — moved from scoping into active development.
Internal target set: have something ready to show GDG for review/revisions by Jul 20.

projects.name: Get Hydrogel — Funnel
projects.nextMilestone: Site + funnel + ads live by Jul 24 → monitor through Aug 7

iterations.note: Project opened: building a sales/marketing funnel for Get Hydrogel.

Scope defined: three deliverables — (1) dedicated funnel website/landing page, (2) GoHighLevel funnel build to capture and route leads, (3) Google Ads campaign driving traffic. Flow: Google Ads -> lead signs up on landing page -> routed to a salesperson -> salesperson closes the lead on investing in hydrogel. This is a lead-gen funnel, not a direct checkout.

Timeline set: website + funnel to be finished within two weeks (by Jul 16), with Google Ads launching by that same date. Jaren (Google Ads) needs to complete keyword research before launch. After launch, monitor ad performance for two weeks (through ~Jul 30) before reviewing results.
Pay confirmed: $5,000/month for this engagement.
Resynced due dates to the actual calendar date — the original Jul 16/30 dates were computed off a wrong 'today.' Build/launch now targets Jul 24, monitoring window runs through Aug 7.

tasks.title: Build dedicated funnel website / landing page
Build GoHighLevel funnel (lead capture + routing to salesperson)
Connect landing page signups to salesperson handoff
Jaren: Google Ads keyword research
Jaren: launch Google Ads campaign
Monitor ad performance (2 weeks)
Review results with team

iterations.note: Project opened: covers both the BYTOX website and their newsletter.

This is an ongoing retainer, not a build project: $2,000/mo, roughly ≤8 hrs/week. Owner requests ad-hoc updates as needed. Recurring deliverables: ~2 newsletters/week, 1-2 SMS blasts/month (2 if there's a sale, 1 if not), plus maintenance of the wholesale website whenever updates are needed.

iterations.note: New business added: Back to Modern, Steven's own e-bike company. First recurring obligation: monthly state tax filings for Florida and Ohio, tracked as two separate tasks since they're filed individually. Due on the 5th of every month — once a month's filing is marked complete, the next month's filing becomes due on the 5th of the following month.
July Florida and Ohio state taxes filed and marked complete. Rolled forward: August filings now open, due Aug 5.
System date resynced from Jul 2/3 to the real Jul 10 — this task's Jul 9 deadline was stated directly by Steven, not computed, so it's left as-is. It now reads as overdue; flagged for follow-up.
